prompt: Create a minimalist vintage Mediterranean editorial poster in a wide 16:9 landscape format. Use a deep cyan-blue matte background with vast negative space, heavy analog film grain, subtle halftone print texture, and slightly aged offset-print color. Center a single botanical subject: a tied bundle of wild Mediterranean herbs, floating horizontally slightly above center, stems pointing to the right and leafy ends spreading to the left. The bundle should include mixed green herbs such as sage leaves, rosemary sprigs, thyme-like fine stems, and small mint-like leaves, bound near the stem end with tan natural twine; render it as realistic but poster-like, with warm sunlight highlights and a soft shadow below. Beneath the herb bundle, place one large cream-colored elegant serif wordmark reading Selvatica, centered near the lower third. Add exactly 2 small matching cream decorative ornaments, one on each side of the title, each made from a short horizontal line, a dot, and another short line. Keep the composition sparse and refined, with no border, no extra objects, no additional text, and no watermark. Overall mood: rustic, botanical, vintage Mediterranean apothecary editorial poster.
